📎 Scope: intentionally restricted to the 94 names already surfaced elsewhere on this tab (movers, 52W watch, gap scanner, RVOL/RSI extremes, RS leaders, Top TA picks, liquidity/risk/volatility leaders) — a volume-focused second pass over your existing filtered output, not a new universe-wide scan.  |  Surge rule: a session is flagged when its volume is ≥ 2× the trailing 20-day Average Daily Volume (ADV20) computed as of that session, with no look-ahead. Adjust VOL_SURGE_MULTIPLIER at the top of the script to recalibrate every surge flag in the dashboard at once.
📎 Universe note: NSE does not publish a free official "Microcap 500" constituent feed, so this tab tracks a curated, broad-based representative basket of actively traded small/micro-cap NSE names spanning 40+ industries as a stand-in for that segment. Replace MICROCAP_500 in the script with a licensed official constituent list if you have one — every other part of the pipeline (indicators, breadth, scanners, TA scoring) is fully generic and needs no changes.

Data Coverage Note
Microcap Universe is tracked with the identical indicator stack as the main dashboard (SMA/EMA, MACD, RSI, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ATR, OBV, VWAP, Supertrend, Ichimoku, Fibonacci, ADX) — 443 names currently resolved out of the configured universe. Micro/small-cap names often have thinner liquidity, so size positions conservatively and favor the Turnover and RVOL columns over raw price moves.

Adjust VOL_SURGE_MULTIPLIER at the top of the script to recalibrate every surge flag in the dashboard at once.

📎 Scope: intentionally restricted to the 61 names already surfaced elsewhere on this tab (movers, 52W watch, gap scanner, RVOL/RSI extremes, RS leaders, Top TA picks, liquidity/risk/volatility leaders) — a volume-focused second pass over your existing filtered output, not a new universe-wide scan.  |  Surge rule: a session is flagged when its volume is ≥ 2× the trailing 20-day Average Daily Volume (ADV20) computed as of that session, with no look-ahead. Adjust VOL_SURGE_MULTIPLIER at the top of the script to recalibrate every surge flag in the dashboard at once.
📝 ETF Notes: Premium/Discount to iNAV requires live iNAV feed from NSE/AMFI.  |  AUM & Expense Ratio: AMFI India ↗  |  TD column = ETF return − index return (tracking difference approximation; true TE requires daily NAV series).  |  Corr = 60-day Pearson correlation of daily returns vs reference index.
Volume-Surge Methodology NEW
⚡ Surge rule: a session is flagged a volume surge when that day's volume is ≥ 2× the trailing 20-day Average Daily Volume (ADV20), computed only from bars strictly before that session (no look-ahead). Change VOL_SURGE_MULTIPLIER near the top of the script to recalibrate every surge flag, badge and report at once.  |  Vol Δ (1D/5D): 1D = today's volume vs. yesterday's; 5D = this week's total volume (last 5 sessions) vs. the preceding 5 sessions — a genuinely cumulative comparison that is more robust to a single noisy day than a simple point-to-point check.  |  Where to find it: the dedicated ⚡ Volume Surge tab covers the main dashboard's filtered output; the Microcap and ETF tabs each carry their own equivalent report at the bottom; every other table across the whole dashboard carries the Vol Δ column, the ⚡ ticker indicator, and — where most relevant (RVOL/gap/liquidity scanners) — a 5-day trailing-volume mini-chart against the ADV baseline.
Data Availability — Additional Professional Signals
The following high-value signals require real-time NSE data feeds not available via public APIs. This dashboard is designed to integrate them when connected to appropriate data sources.
📊 FII/DII Net Flows BSE/NSE
Foreign Institutional & Domestic Institutional net buy/sell figures. Strong leading indicator for large-cap direction.
↗ NSE FII/DII Data
🎲 Put/Call Ratio (PCR) F&O
Options market sentiment indicator. PCR >1.3 = bullish contrarian; PCR <0.7 = bearish contrarian.
↗ NSE Derivatives
📦 Delivery % (DII Proxy) NSE
Delivery % = stocks actually taken vs intraday churn. High delivery → genuine accumulation.
↗ NSE Deliverable Data
📉 Index Contribution Weights
Top 10 Nifty 50 stocks contribute ~55% of index moves.
↗ Nifty 50 Weights
⚡ Market Open Auction Real-Time
Pre-open session (9:00–9:15 AM IST) IEP signals. Key for gap strategy setup before market opens.
🌐 Global Macro Overlay Context
USD/INR, Crude Oil (Brent), US Fed Funds Rate, 10Y US Treasury yield, SGX Nifty futures.

🧠 Neural-Trail Confidence Engine — Methodology (fully transparent, no black box)
1. Setup Score (0–100): a weighted composite of Trend (EMA20/50/200 stack, ADX/DI, Supertrend), Momentum (RSI triangular zone centred on 55, MACD histogram sign+slope, Stochastic), Volume (RVOL, 5-day surge trail, OBV trend) and Price-Action (bullish engulfing, hammer, marubozu, inside-bar breakout, HH-HL structure, gap-up-on-volume). A BUY setup requires Setup Score ≥ 60, ATR% between 1.0%–8.0% and 20-day avg turnover ≥ ₹3 Cr (liquidity floor). The identical rule is applied bar-by-bar across all 5 years of history to generate the backtest trades below — today's signal is validated by, not separate from, the historical test.  |  2. 5-Year Backtest: every historical day the rule fired, a trade is simulated — entry at the next session's open (no look-ahead), stoploss at the nearest confirmed fractal swing-low support minus a 0.3% buffer, target fixed at 1:3 risk-reward, exit at whichever of stoploss / target / 10-day time-stop comes first (a same-day stoploss+target hit is conservatively scored as the stoploss). Only one open trade per stock at a time.  |  3. Confidence Level (0–100%): a transparent single-layer weighted-and-sigmoid-squashed score (technically a 1-layer neural/logistic scoring function — not a deep-trained black box) blending today's Setup Score, the backtest win-rate after a Wilson-score shrinkage for sample size (so 3 lucky trades can't outrank 40 consistent ones), the raw trade count, whether the nearest resistance leaves realistic room for the fixed 3R target, live RVOL confirmation, today's price-action strength, and the stock's 5-day return relative to its own sector's average (a stock beating its sector by ≥5pp over 5 days gets full credit here; one lagging by ≥5pp gets none — rewarding names showing genuine leadership within their group, not just riding a sector-wide wave). Weights are fixed and fully disclosed: bias=-3.4, setup=+2.2, winrate=+2.0, sample=+1.0, rr_realism=+1.0, volume=+0.8, pattern=+0.6, relstrength=+0.7.  |  Reading the win-rate: at a fixed 1:3 risk-reward, the mathematical break-even win-rate is only 25% (win% × 3R = loss% × 1R). A stock showing a 45–60% historical win-rate here is not a weak signal — it implies a materially positive expectancy per trade before costs, which is why Confidence Level weighs the win-rate alongside sample size and setup quality rather than by itself.

⚠ Data Quality, Scope & Risk Disclosure
Universe = the Nifty 500 list used elsewhere on this dashboard, unioned with the Microcap basket used on the Microcap 500 tab (754 unique symbols after de-duplication). Each symbol is fetched and analysed independently on 5Y of daily history; symbols with under 300 trading days of history (recent listings) are excluded rather than guessed at. This module fetches its own 5y OHLCV series and computes its own indicator stack completely independently of the rest of the dashboard (which uses 2y of history) — nothing on any other tab is altered by this feature.  |  Backtest limitations: no brokerage, STT, slippage or market-impact cost is modelled beyond the stoploss buffer; 5 years of one regime is not a guarantee of forward performance.  |  Liquidity at scale: Total Trades / Win-Rate / Confidence are computed per stock in isolation. Deploying very large capital into microcap names on this list WILL move the price — size positions against the 20-day average turnover shown, not against account size, and expect wider slippage below ₹10–15 Cr/day turnover.  |  This is a systematic, rules-based screen, not investment advice — apply independent judgement, portfolio-level risk limits and position sizing, and consult a SEBI-registered advisor before committing capital.

🚦 Nifty 50 Market Bias — "based on Nifty, take the call"
Today's read: Bullish  (bias score 100/100, Nifty 50 5-day return +0.5%). This is folded into every stock's Confidence % below: a Bullish tape nudges BUY/BTST confidence up and SELL confidence down (and the reverse in a Bearish tape) — never overriding the stock's own setup and backtest, only tilting the blend, on the principle that fighting the index on a single-day or overnight trade is a real edge-reducer.

 Green CandleFresh Volume SurgeStrong Body vs Wick>5% Below ATH
📐 Methodology — why daily bars, not tick data (fully disclosed)
A genuine 5-year intraday (minute-level) backtest is not feasible here — free-tier data providers, including the one this dashboard uses, only retain intraday bars for a recent rolling window (days to a few months), never 5 years. Rather than quietly fabricate a precision this scan doesn't have, here is exactly what is simulated instead: an Intraday trade enters at the next session's open and MUST exit that same session — target/stoploss are counted as hit if that day's High/Low actually reached the level, otherwise the position is force-squared-off at that day's Close (exactly like a real intraday MIS position). A BTST trade enters at today's Close and may be exited any time up to tomorrow's Close — an opening gap straight through the level, an intraday High/Low touch, or a forced exit at tomorrow's Close if neither triggers. Both use the real High/Low of the relevant session, so the simulation is not blind to intraday extremes — it simply cannot know the exact intraday timing/path, which is exactly why this is disclosed here rather than presented as tick-precise. Fixed risk-reward: 1:2.5 for Intraday and BTST alike. Same-rule guarantee: the identical entry rule generates both today's live call and every historical backtest trade below — SELL (short) setups use a fully mirrored bearish scoring system (bearish engulfing, shooting star, bearish marubozu, LL-LH structure, gap-down-on-volume) rather than an inverted BUY score. Confidence % blends the Wilson-shrunk win-rate (sample size aware), the day's own Setup Score, resistance/support realism against the fixed target, live volume confirmation, price-action strength, and the Nifty 50 bias described above.  |  BTST uses a different, rule-based (not composite-scored) gate — all five must hold: (1) green candle, (2) today's volume is a fresh surge — surging now but not on any of the preceding 5 sessions, (3) (Close−Open)/2 > (High−Close) — a strong body with a small upper wick, (4) last price is more than 5% below the all-time high seen in this scan's ~5-year window (not necessarily the stock's entire listed history), and (5) (High−Low)/Close×100 is under 12%. The Top 5 BTST list is then sorted by volume-surge magnitude (RVOL) descending, then by today's price change ascending — the biggest fresh surge that hasn't already fully played out in price — rather than by Confidence %, which is still shown alongside for context but is not the BTST sort key.

Simple BTST Backtest (Top 5):
⚠ Data Quality, Scope & Risk Disclosure
Universe = Nifty 500 ∪ Microcap basket used elsewhere on this dashboard (754 unique symbols); a stricter ₹8 Cr/day liquidity floor applies here versus the Swing tab's ₹3 Cr, since same-day or next-day exits need real depth to fill without heavy slippage — illiquid names simply will not clear the gate, regardless of how good the setup looks. No brokerage, STT, exchange/SEBI charges, or slippage are modelled beyond the stoploss buffer; intraday round-trips carry meaningfully higher cost-drag per trade than swing trades, which is not reflected in the raw win-rate shown. This is a systematic, rules-based screen on daily bars, not investment advice and not a substitute for a real-time Level-2/tick feed — apply independent judgement and position sizing, and consult a SEBI-registered advisor before committing capital, intraday leverage in particular.
NSE Nifty 500 Pro
⚠ Data sourced from Yahoo Finance (yfinance), with a synthetic fallback used only when a live fetch fails. For informational purposes only. This report does not constitute investment advice. Consult a SEBI-registered advisor before trading. Past performance does not guarantee future results. All prices are as of last available close.
Report generated: 20 Jul 2026 12:26:24 PM IST · NSE Nifty 500 Professional Dashboard v8 (Tabbed / ETF Edition) · Powered by Python + yfinance
